Instructions read. Summary of my role in this session:

**Shop:** shopsystem product (lead shop). No implementation code here — code lives in sibling BCs under `repos/`.

**Router role:** I classify requests and dispatch to subagents; I do not enact PO/Architect roles myself.
- `lead-po` — Gherkin scenarios, briefs/PDRs, clarify on scope/vocabulary.
- `lead-architect` — message-type vehicle selection, `shop-msg send`, BC pre-state verification, clarify on architecture, register reconciliation, ADRs, decomposition.
- Main-agent context — routine git/beads/shell, state reporting, conversational clarification.

**Standing rules for Monitor events** (`<work_id> <message_type>`):
- `work_done` → lead-architect (reconciliation)
- `clarify` → lead-po (scope/vocab) or lead-architect (architecture); default lead-architect if ambiguous
- `mechanism_observation` → lead-architect

**Session start protocol:**
1. Arm Monitor on `shop-msg watch --lead <name>`
2. Drain pending: `shop-msg pending outbox --lead <name>` and `shop-msg pending inbox --lead <name>`
3. Dispatch per standing rule before accepting user work

**Task tracking:** beads (`bd`), not TodoWrite/markdown. `bd ready`, `bd show`, `bd update --claim`, `bd close`. `bd remember` for persistent knowledge — not MEMORY.md.

**CWD trap:** pin all bd/shop-msg/git to `/workspaces/shopsystem-product`; sibling repos have their own bd.

**Session close:** `git status` → `git add` → `git commit` → `bd dolt push` → `git push` → verify up-to-date.

**Known noise:** Monitor events for non-bd work_ids are shopsystem-messaging BDD test fixture pollution — ignore until lead-0bw lands.

Ready. Want me to arm the Monitor watcher and run the session-start drain?
